title: gouldia axillaris var. nodosa
intro: Gouldia axillaris var. nodosa, also known as the nodous axillary gouldia, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being. Gouldia axillaris var. nodosa is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the plant's active compounds with a solvent.
